project.name: MLEvolve
project.description: LLM-driven ML solution evolution agent: tree-search over code candidates (draft/improve/debug/fusion/evolution), execute + evaluate each. Scheduler excluded from this graph.

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: VS Code devcontainer image built from Microsoft's Anaconda base image; conditionally updates the conda base environment from environment.yml when present, providing the project's Python development sandbox.
nodes.languageNotes: Single-stage devcontainer Dockerfile using a COPY glob (environment.yml*) plus a noop.txt fallback so the COPY does not fail when environment.yml is absent; conda env update runs only inside a guarded shell conditional.

nodes.name: anaconda
nodes.summary: Build stage based on mcr.microsoft.com/devcontainers/anaconda:1-3 that installs the project's conda environment for interactive development.

nodes.name: devcontainer.json
nodes.summary: VS Code Dev Container 配置，基于本地 Dockerfile 构建 Anaconda Python 3 开发环境，启用 docker-outside-of-docker、nvtop 与 pytorch 等 features，并以 --gpus=all 和 32g 共享内存运行容器以支持 GPU 训练。
nodes.languageNotes: runArgs 中的 --gpus=all 与 --shm-size=32g 是 PyTorch GPU 工作负载的常见容器参数；features 通过 OCI registry (ghcr.io) 引用可复用的 devcontainer 组件。

nodes.name: devcontainer-lock.json
nodes.summary: Dev Container features 的锁定文件，将 devcontainer.json 引用的三个 feature（docker-outside-of-docker、nvtop、pytorch）固定到具体版本与 sha256 摘要，确保开发环境构建可复现。

nodes.name: noop.txt
nodes.summary: 占位文件，与父目录的 environment.yml* 一起被 COPY 进容器；其作用是在没有 environment.yml 时防止 Dockerfile 的 COPY 指令因找不到源文件而失败。

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Agent guidance file holding project-specific rules for Claude Code, including schema-writing conventions and GPU/VRAM constraints for experiments.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Project overview for MLEvolve, an agentic ML engineering system using Monte Carlo Graph Search; covers MLE-bench results, the hardware-aware agent integration, setup, and quick-start instructions.

nodes.name: TODO.md
nodes.summary: Roadmap notes tracking planned work on novelty positioning, the agent system plugin, cluster-side graph DB and data accumulation, and log database.

nodes.name: requirements_base.txt
nodes.summary: Base Python dependency pins grouped by role: core runtime, LLM/embedding clients, scheduler/graph/vector/memory backends, and validation/benchmark tooling including mle-bench.

nodes.name: config.example.yaml
nodes.summary: Example OmegaConf configuration template covering data paths, experiment/search settings, monitor, executor, scheduler bridge, agent stages, and cold-start options for an MLEvolve run.
nodes.languageNotes: Single deeply-nested YAML mapping mirroring the dataclass config hierarchy in config/__init__.py (Config -> ExperimentConfig, AgentConfig, ExecConfig, SchedulerBridgeConfig, etc.).

nodes.name: docker-compose.local.yml
nodes.summary: Local Docker Compose stack provisioning the Qdrant vector database and Neo4j graph database backends used by MLEvolve's memory and scheduler context layers.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses env-var port defaults (e.g. ${QDRANT_HTTP_PORT:-6333}) and a cypher-shell healthcheck for Neo4j readiness.

nodes.name: qdrant
nodes.summary: Qdrant v1.12.6 vector database service exposing HTTP (6333) and gRPC (6334) ports with a persistent storage volume.

nodes.name: neo4j
nodes.summary: Neo4j 5.26 graph database service exposing HTTP (7474) and Bolt (7687) ports with auth, persistent data/log volumes, and a cypher-shell healthcheck.

nodes.name: bootstrap.sh
nodes.summary: Container/runtime bootstrap script that reads config values, starts local Qdrant and Neo4j databases, waits for them to become ready, and ingests schema knowledge before launching the agent.
nodes.languageNotes: Defines readiness-polling helpers (wait_for_qdrant, wait_for_neo4j) and a cleanup trap; orchestrates the local backend stack at startup.

nodes.name: read_config_value
nodes.summary: Reads a single value from the runtime YAML/config, used to derive ports and connection settings during bootstrap.

nodes.name: write_runtime_config
nodes.summary: Generates the runtime configuration file with resolved database endpoints and credentials for the agent process.

nodes.name: start_local_databases
nodes.summary: Brings up the local Qdrant and Neo4j containers via Docker Compose when running outside a managed cluster.

nodes.name: wait_for_qdrant
nodes.summary: Polls the Qdrant HTTP endpoint until the vector database reports ready or a retry budget is exhausted.

nodes.name: vector_db_is_empty
nodes.summary: Inspects Qdrant collections to determine whether the vector store is empty and therefore needs initial schema-knowledge ingestion.

nodes.name: wait_for_neo4j
nodes.summary: Polls Neo4j via cypher-shell until the graph database accepts queries or the retry budget is exhausted.

nodes.name: ingest_schema_knowledge
nodes.summary: Triggers ingestion of schema knowledge into the vector and graph databases when they are empty.

nodes.name: compare_hardware_awareness.sh
nodes.summary: Experiment driver that runs MLEvolve in baseline vs hardware-aware modes on a task, managing the validation server, dataset preparation, and comparison plot generation.
nodes.languageNotes: Defines run_mode for parameterized baseline/hardware-aware launches and generate_plots for post-run comparison visualization; large argument-parsing usage block.

nodes.name: usage
nodes.summary: Prints the detailed CLI usage and flag reference for the hardware-awareness comparison driver.

nodes.name: prepare_dataset
nodes.summary: Prepares the competition dataset (download/extract into the expected layout) before the comparison runs unless skipped.

nodes.name: wait_for_validation_server
nodes.summary: Polls the format/grading validation server health endpoint until it is online before launching experiments.

nodes.name: start_validation_server
nodes.summary: Launches the submission format-validation server in the background for the duration of the comparison run.

nodes.name: run_mode
nodes.summary: Runs run.py in a given experiment mode (baseline or hardware_aware) with the resolved task, dataset, step, and timeout settings.

nodes.name: generate_plots
nodes.summary: Generates comparison plots of baseline vs hardware-aware runs after both experiment modes complete.

nodes.name: docker_host_databases.sh
nodes.summary: Host-side helper that starts, waits on, and reports status of the Qdrant and Neo4j Docker containers (standalone or via the local compose stack) for development outside the devcontainer.
nodes.languageNotes: Abstracts docker compose vs docker run, with container_exists/container_running guards and readiness polling for both backends.

nodes.name: usage
nodes.summary: Prints CLI usage and subcommand reference for the host database management helper.

nodes.name: require_docker
nodes.summary: Verifies the Docker CLI is available and the daemon is reachable before attempting container operations.

nodes.name: start_qdrant
nodes.summary: Starts a standalone Qdrant container with the project port mapping and persistent volume.

nodes.name: start_neo4j
nodes.summary: Starts a standalone Neo4j container with auth, port mappings, and persistent data/log volumes.

nodes.name: wait_for_qdrant
nodes.summary: Polls the Qdrant HTTP endpoint until the container reports ready.

nodes.name: neo4j_cypher
nodes.summary: Executes a Cypher query against the running Neo4j container via cypher-shell.

nodes.name: wait_for_neo4j
nodes.summary: Polls Neo4j via cypher-shell until the container accepts queries.

nodes.name: print_devcontainer_hint
nodes.summary: Prints guidance on how to connect the started host databases from inside the devcontainer.

nodes.name: install_dependencies.sh
nodes.summary: Dependency installer that ensures Git LFS and Python are present, installs the base requirements, and verifies key package imports succeed.
nodes.languageNotes: verify_imports runs a Python smoke test importing critical packages to fail fast on broken installs.

nodes.name: install_git_lfs
nodes.summary: Detects the package manager and installs Git LFS (Large File Storage) when it is not already present.

nodes.name: verify_imports
nodes.summary: Runs a Python smoke test importing critical runtime, LLM, and backend packages to confirm the install is functional.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Entry-point orchestrating the dependency installation flow: Git LFS, Python check, pip install, and import verification.

nodes.name: nautilus.sh
nodes.summary: Nautilus/Kubernetes deployment script that creates the DB secret, applies Qdrant/Neo4j manifests, runs the knowledge-ingest job, and launches the MLEvolve job.
nodes.languageNotes: Pure kubectl invocations applying k8s/nautilus manifests with readiness waits; no shell functions.

nodes.name: run_single_task.sh
nodes.summary: Launches MLEvolve on a single competition task: starts the grading/validation server, resolves config and experiment mode, runs run.py under a CUDA device, then ensembles top solutions.
nodes.languageNotes: Resolves MLEVOLVE_CONFIG (config.yaml fallback to config.example.yaml) and invokes run.py with OmegaConf-style key=value overrides.

nodes.name: start.sh
nodes.summary: Convenience launcher that sets Qdrant/Neo4j endpoints and invokes the hardware-awareness comparison driver on the histopathologic-cancer-detection task with preset flags.

nodes.name: dependabot.yml
nodes.summary: Dependabot configuration that schedules automated dependency update pull requests for the repository.

nodes.name: .understandignore
nodes.summary: Ignore list scoping the knowledge-graph analysis to the core MLEvolve engine (engine/, agents/, llm/, config/, run.py) while excluding the scheduler, tests, docs, and schema tooling.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Top-level package entry point defining the Solution dataclass and the Experiment orchestrator that loads config, prepares the workspace, and runs the agent search to return the best solution.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a lazy ModuleNotFoundError fallback import pattern for optional dependencies.

nodes.name: Experiment
nodes.summary: Orchestrator that initializes config, journal, agent and interpreter, then runs the search loop for a number of steps and extracts the best node as a Solution.

nodes.name: aggregation_agent.py
nodes.summary: Aggregation agent that collects top-performing representative nodes across search branches and synthesizes a unified solution via an LLM plan-and-code query.

nodes.name: _collect_branch_representatives
nodes.summary: Selects the best-scoring node from each successful search branch and returns them sorted by metric value as aggregation candidates.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Builds reference summaries from branch representatives, injects hardware context, queries the coder LLM, and registers the aggregated solution as a new search node.

nodes.name: code_review_agent.py
nodes.summary: Code review agent that prompts an LLM to critique and revise a node's code, applying search/replace patches when revisions are returned.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Constructs the review prompt with hardware context, calls the LLM with retries, parses the verdict, and applies revised code via the search/replace patcher.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Coder subpackage barrel that re-exports the plan-and-code query entry points for base and stepwise coders.

nodes.name: base_coder.py
nodes.summary: Base coder that issues a single plan-and-code LLM query with retries, extracting the generated code and accompanying plan text.

nodes.name: plan_and_code_query
nodes.summary: Generates code from a prompt with retry logic, extracting the code block and the preceding plan text from the LLM response.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Diff-coder subpackage barrel re-exporting the diff generation, application, patcher, and prompt-building utilities.

nodes.name: apply.py
nodes.summary: Applies LLM-generated search/replace diffs to source code with validation and retry-via-regeneration when the diff format or match fails.

nodes.name: apply_diff_with_retry
nodes.summary: Validates diff block markers, applies the search/replace patch, and on failure regenerates the diff up to a retry limit before raising.

nodes.name: diff_generate.py
nodes.summary: Builds the diff-mode prompt from a planning result and parent code, queries the LLM, and applies the resulting diff with retry support.

nodes.name: diff_generate_and_apply
nodes.summary: Assembles diff instructions, user and assistant context, generates the diff via the LLM, and applies it through apply_diff_with_retry returning the patched code.

nodes.name: _build_assistant_text
nodes.summary: Assembles the assistant-turn context block from data preview, prior code, execution output, and extra context for the diff prompt.

nodes.name: _format_plan_text
nodes.summary: Formats a structured planning result into human-readable plan text for inclusion in the diff prompt.

nodes.name: patcher.py
nodes.summary: Search/replace patcher that applies diff blocks to source code with whitespace normalization, indentation-aware matching, and fuzzy diff fallback diagnostics.

nodes.name: SearchReplacePatcher
nodes.summary: Applies SEARCH/REPLACE patch blocks against original text, with helpers for trailing-whitespace stripping, indented matching, indentation transfer, and closest-match diff reporting on failure.

nodes.name: prompts.py
nodes.summary: Provides the base diff-mode instructions and the SEARCH/REPLACE format suffix used when prompting the LLM to produce code diffs.

nodes.name: build_base_diff_instructions
nodes.summary: Builds the base set of diff-mode instruction strings, optionally appending learning guidance for the LLM.

nodes.name: stepwise_coder.py
nodes.summary: Stepwise coder that decomposes solution generation into specialized step agents (data, model, training) whose outputs a meta-agent merges into a single coherent program, with optional hardware-aware reasoning.

nodes.name: StepAgent
nodes.summary: A single stepwise generation agent that builds a stage-specific prompt incorporating prior code and memory, then generates a code fragment via the LLM.

nodes.name: MetaAgent
nodes.summary: Merges the code fragments produced by step agents into one cohesive program via an LLM merge prompt, falling back to simple concatenation on failure.

nodes.name: StepwiseContext
nodes.summary: Dataclass carrying per-step context such as stage, memory, previous code, execution output, and hardware design brief.

nodes.name: create_default_step_agents
nodes.summary: Constructs the default ordered set of StepAgents (data, model, training) with optional hardware-aware guidelines.

nodes.name: stepwise_plan_and_code_query
nodes.summary: Drives the stepwise pipeline: runs each step agent in sequence then merges their outputs via the meta-agent into the final code and plan.

nodes.name: data_leakage_agent.py
nodes.summary: Data leakage detection agent that prompts an LLM to inspect a node's code for train/test leakage and records the verdict.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Wraps the node code into a leakage-inspection prompt, queries the LLM, parses the leakage verdict, and logs the outcome.

nodes.name: debug_agent.py
nodes.summary: Debug agent that diagnoses a failing node's execution error, retrieves similar past fixes from global memory, and generates a corrected solution via diff or full-code regeneration.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Builds the debug prompt with error context, hardware hints, and retrieved memory guidance, then generates and applies a fix (via patcher or coder) and registers the resulting node.

nodes.name: _format_debug_memory_guidance
nodes.summary: Formats retrieved similar-fix memory records into a guidance text block to steer the debug LLM toward known solutions.

nodes.name: draft_agent.py
nodes.summary: Draft agent that produces an initial solution from scratch (or a seed file), applying a hardware design brief and dispatching to either the stepwise or single-shot coder.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Assembles the draft prompt with implementation guidelines and hardware brief, generates initial code via stepwise or base coder, and registers the new root-level search node.

nodes.name: evolution_agent.py
nodes.summary: Evolution agent that triggers larger architectural changes when a branch stagnates, building an evolution prompt from the branch trajectory and applying diff-based code changes.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Gathers branch trajectory and stagnation signals, builds the evolution prompt with hardware context, generates the evolved solution (diff or full), and registers the node.

nodes.name: _diff_evolution
nodes.summary: Runs the planner then applies a diff-based evolution change via diff_generate_and_apply, returning the new code and plan.

nodes.name: _get_branch_trajectory_for_evolution
nodes.summary: Extracts the root-to-current branch trajectory text used as evolution context, guarding against empty trajectories.

nodes.name: fusion_agent.py
nodes.summary: Fusion agent that combines two or more high-performing branch solutions into a single node, supporting pairwise and multi-reference fusion via diff or full-code generation.

nodes.name: fuse_two_nodes
nodes.summary: Fuses a source and target node by building a combined prompt with both trajectories and hardware context, generating the merged solution and registering it as a new node.

nodes.name: _fuse_with_multiple_references
nodes.summary: Fuses a parent node with multiple reference solutions by assembling all reference summaries into a multi-fusion prompt and generating the combined node.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Selects fusion candidates and dispatches to pairwise or multi-reference fusion depending on how many candidates are available.

nodes.name: _diff_fusion
nodes.summary: Runs the planner then applies a pairwise diff-based fusion via diff_generate_and_apply, returning the fused code and plan.

nodes.name: _diff_multi_fusion
nodes.summary: Runs the planner then applies a multi-reference diff-based fusion via diff_generate_and_apply, returning the fused code and plan.

nodes.name: _get_fusion_candidates
nodes.summary: Collects the top nodes across branches as candidate solutions to be fused.

nodes.name: hardware_context.py
nodes.summary: Central hardware-awareness layer that queries the scheduler for VRAM/runtime profiles, compacts the evidence, and formats hardware design briefs and prompt sections injected into every agent stage and search node.
nodes.languageNotes: Large module of mostly small pure formatting/compaction helpers fronted by a handful of public entry points.

nodes.name: HardwarePromptContext
nodes.summary: Dataclass bundling a hardware candidate, raw and compacted scheduler context, and the formatted prompt section, with a found() predicate.

nodes.name: get_hardware_design_brief
nodes.summary: Builds the model-design hardware brief for the draft stage by inferring workload type and querying the scheduler for model-design and optimization context.

nodes.name: get_hardware_context_for_stage
nodes.summary: Builds a hardware candidate and queries the scheduler for stage-specific optimization context, returning a compacted HardwarePromptContext for prompt injection.

nodes.name: optimize_training_parameters_for_round
nodes.summary: Builds hardware candidates for a batch of nodes and rewrites their training parameters (batch size, epochs) to fit the available hardware budget.

nodes.name: build_hardware_candidate
nodes.summary: Constructs a hardware candidate descriptor from agent state, stage, parent node and code for submission to the scheduler.

nodes.name: format_hardware_design_brief
nodes.summary: Renders the compacted model-design context into a bounded human-readable hardware design brief for the draft prompt.

nodes.name: format_hardware_prompt_section
nodes.summary: Renders the compacted optimization context into a bounded hardware prompt section listing profiles, VRAM budget, and resource hints.

nodes.name: compact_model_design_context
nodes.summary: Compacts the raw scheduler model-design context into a token-efficient structure for prompt rendering.

nodes.name: _compact_hardware_context
nodes.summary: Compacts the full hardware context object by reducing graph evidence, profiles, diagnosis, and code-knowledge subsections.

nodes.name: apply_hardware_context_to_node
nodes.summary: Copies the compacted hardware context (recommended batch size, epochs, VRAM budget, backend) from a HardwarePromptContext onto a search node for downstream use.

nodes.name: improve_agent.py
nodes.summary: Improve agent that incrementally refines an existing parent solution, building an improvement prompt with memory and hardware context and applying diff-based code changes.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Constructs the improvement prompt from the parent node, generates a refined solution via diff or full-code coder, and registers the new child node.

nodes.name: _diff_improve
nodes.summary: Runs the planner then applies a diff-based improvement via diff_generate_and_apply, returning the improved code and plan.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Memory subpackage barrel re-exporting the global memory layer and related memory components.

nodes.name: embedding_models.py
nodes.summary: Embedding model wrapper providing batched text encoding, including an OpenRouter HTTP embeddings backend used by the memory retrieval layer.

nodes.name: EmbeddingModel
nodes.summary: Encodes text into embedding vectors with batching, including a private OpenRouter REST backend with JSON POST helpers.

nodes.name: global_memory.py
nodes.summary: Global memory layer that persists successful and buggy node records, builds an embedding index, retrieves similar past records, and generates guidance prompts for agents.

nodes.name: GlobalMemoryLayer
nodes.summary: Manages cross-run memory: saves qualifying nodes as records, maintains the retrieval index, returns similar records, and synthesizes guidance prompts from stored experience.

nodes.name: record.py
nodes.summary: Defines the MemRecord dataclass representing a single stored memory entry with serialization to and from dict.

nodes.name: MemRecord
nodes.summary: Dataclass for a memory record (id, title, description, method, label, timestamp) with from_dict and to_dict serialization helpers.

nodes.name: retriever.py
nodes.summary: Hybrid retriever that combines dense embedding search with sparse lexical matching via reciprocal rank fusion, persisting and loading the index for memory retrieval.

nodes.name: HybridRetriever
nodes.summary: Builds, persists and queries a hybrid dense+sparse retrieval index, fusing ranked results with reciprocal rank fusion to return the most relevant memory records.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Barrel for the planner package; re-exports the generic planner pipeline (base_planner) and the memory-enhanced two-stage planner (planner_with_memory) used by all diff-based agents.
nodes.languageNotes: Python package barrel using relative re-exports.

nodes.name: base_planner.py
nodes.summary: Generic single-stage planning pipeline: builds chat/model prompts, runs the planner agent with retry, and parses/normalizes the LLM planning response into a validated module-selection JSON.

nodes.name: parse_planning_response
nodes.summary: Parses the raw LLM planning response into a structured result, extracting and validating the embedded JSON.

nodes.name: _normalize_planning_result
nodes.summary: Normalizes a parsed planning result, coercing module selections into the allowed schema and filling defaults.

nodes.name: _clean_json_control_chars
nodes.summary: Strips invalid control characters from JSON text so the planning response can be safely parsed.

nodes.name: build_model_prompt
nodes.summary: Builds a model-specific completion prompt from an introduction, user prompt and assistant suffix.

nodes.name: build_chat_prompt_for_model
nodes.summary: Builds a chat-format prompt for a given model with an assistant prefix.

nodes.name: build_planner_suffix
nodes.summary: Assembles the planner prompt suffix from the prompt base, data preview, context and extra text.

nodes.name: run_planner
nodes.summary: Core planner driver: builds the full prompt, invokes the agent with retry handling, and returns the parsed/normalized planning result.

nodes.name: planner_with_memory.py
nodes.summary: Memory-enhanced two-stage planner: first drafts an initial plan, then refines it into validated JSON using guidance derived from similar past success/failure records.

nodes.name: generate_initial_plan
nodes.summary: Stage one: prompts the agent to produce an initial free-form module plan from the prompt base, data preview and context.

nodes.name: refine_plan_to_json
nodes.summary: Stage two: refines the initial plan into validated module-selection JSON, incorporating memory-derived refinement guidance.

nodes.name: _build_refinement_guidance
nodes.summary: Builds refinement guidance text from similar successful and failed past records.

nodes.name: _build_refine_user_prompt
nodes.summary: Constructs the user prompt for the refinement stage from the base prompt, initial plan, guidance and component descriptions.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Barrel for the shared prompt package; re-exports leakage-prevention, response-format, environment and implementation-guideline prompt builders.

nodes.name: environment.py
nodes.summary: Provides the prompt fragment describing the execution environment (available packages, hardware) injected into agent prompts.

nodes.name: get_prompt_environment
nodes.summary: Returns the environment-description prompt fragment listing the runtime libraries and constraints available to generated code.

nodes.name: impl_guideline.py
nodes.summary: Builds the implementation-guideline prompt covering remaining time/steps, execution timeout, prediction exposure and k-fold validation rules for the coding agent.

nodes.name: get_impl_guideline
nodes.summary: Constructs the implementation guideline prompt from time/steps budget, timeout and validation flags.

nodes.name: get_impl_guideline_from_agent
nodes.summary: Convenience wrapper that derives implementation-guideline parameters from an agent instance and delegates to get_impl_guideline.

nodes.name: shared.py
nodes.summary: Shared prompt fragments: data-leakage prevention rules, response-format instructions and internet/pretrained-model usage clarifications reused across agents.

nodes.name: get_internet_clarification
nodes.summary: Returns prompt text clarifying internet access and local pretrained-model directory usage for generated code.

nodes.name: prompt_leakage_prevention
nodes.summary: Returns the prompt fragment instructing the agent to avoid data leakage between train and validation/test.

nodes.name: validation_template_prompts.py
nodes.summary: Templates for the code-review/validation agent: builds the review prompt, the reviewer guidelines, and the structured response-format specification.

nodes.name: get_code_review_prompt
nodes.summary: Builds the code-review prompt combining the task description and the candidate code.

nodes.name: get_code_review_guidelines
nodes.summary: Returns the detailed reviewer guideline text used to judge generated solution code.

nodes.name: get_code_review_response_format
nodes.summary: Returns the structured response-format specification the reviewer must follow.

nodes.name: result_parse_agent.py
nodes.summary: Result-parsing/validation agent: inspects an executed node's output, determines metric direction, validates submission format and content quality, checks for data leakage, and persists outcomes to global memory.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Top-level entry that validates an executed node end-to-end: submission check, format validation, content quality, metric direction, leakage check and memory persistence.

nodes.name: determine_metric_direction
nodes.summary: Determines whether the task metric should be maximized or minimized, querying the agent/LLM when ambiguous.

nodes.name: get_review_func_spec
nodes.summary: Returns the function/tool spec describing the structured review output, optionally including memory fields.

nodes.name: _validate_format_with_retry
nodes.summary: Validates the submission output format against expectations, retrying parsing on failure.

nodes.name: _check_data_leakage
nodes.summary: Checks the node's code/response for potential train-test data leakage and records the finding.

nodes.name: triggers.py
nodes.summary: Search-control trigger helpers: decides when to check data leakage, computes the debug patience counter, and registers new nodes into the search journal.

nodes.name: get_patience_counter
nodes.summary: Computes the remaining debug patience for a node based on its parent and configured limits.

nodes.name: register_node
nodes.summary: Registers a newly created node into the search tree, wiring parent/child links and branch metadata.

nodes.name: should_check_data_leakage
nodes.summary: Decides whether the data-leakage validation should run for the given node.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Central configuration package: defines the dataclass hierarchy (Config and nested Stage/Search/Agent/Exec/Scheduler/etc. configs) and loaders that resolve, prepare and persist run configuration.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses dataclass-based nested config schema with OmegaConf-style loading.

nodes.name: Config
nodes.summary: Root configuration aggregating data directories, eval goal, exec, scheduler, experiment, agent, coldstart and monitor sub-configs for a full MLEvolve run.

nodes.name: SearchConfig
nodes.summary: Tree-search hyperparameters: debug depth/probability, draft/improve counts, branch stagnation thresholds, exploration decay and fusion triggers.

nodes.name: AgentConfig
nodes.summary: Agent-level settings: step/time budgets, memory usage, diff mode, and hardware-context options, embedding nested search and decay configs.

nodes.name: SchedulerBridgeConfig
nodes.summary: Settings for bridging to the external GPU scheduler service: enablement, settings path, runtime root and preload-source model identifiers.

nodes.name: MonitorConfig
nodes.summary: Resource-monitor settings: polling interval, GPU/CPU idle thresholds and adaptive CSV compression parameters.

nodes.name: ColdstartConfig
nodes.summary: Cold-start settings pointing at task/model knowledge-base JSON paths and an optional description.

nodes.name: prep_cfg
nodes.summary: Prepares a loaded config: resolves paths, derives log index/workspace and normalizes nested fields before a run.

nodes.name: resolve_config_path
nodes.summary: Resolves a config file path against known config locations, returning an absolute path.

nodes.name: load_task_desc
nodes.summary: Loads the task description text for the configured experiment from the dataset directory.

nodes.name: save_run
nodes.summary: Persists the run's journal and config artifacts to the run log directory.

nodes.name: agent_search.py
nodes.summary: Defines AgentSearch, the top-level MCTS-style search driver that steps through node selection, planning, execution and backpropagation while coordinating the GPU scheduler and hardware context.

nodes.name: AgentSearch
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the tree search loop: selects nodes, runs single/deferred steps, attaches the scheduler, refreshes hardware context and manages selectable work across branches.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Barrel for the cold-start package; re-exports guidance-building helpers that turn task/model knowledge bases into model recommendations.

nodes.name: classify_tasks.py
nodes.summary: Offline utility that classifies competition tasks into tags by calling an LLM, with retry handling, and writes the classified-tag knowledge base.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry that iterates configured tasks, classifies each via the LLM client and persists the resulting tag mapping JSON.

nodes.name: classify_task
nodes.summary: Classifies a single task into competition tags using the LLM client and the task description.

nodes.name: _single_call
nodes.summary: Performs one LLM classification call for a task with bounded retries.

nodes.name: competition_tag_classified.json
nodes.summary: Cold-start knowledge base mapping competition/task names to classification tags, consumed when building model guidance for new tasks.

nodes.name: knowledge.py
nodes.summary: Cold-start guidance builder: loads task/model knowledge JSONs, collects available pretrained models for a task and assembles the guidance description text and init-solution paths.

nodes.name: collect_models_for_task
nodes.summary: Collects candidate pretrained models relevant to a task from the task/model knowledge bases.

nodes.name: _build_guidance_text
nodes.summary: Builds the human-readable model-guidance text for a task from its matched models, filtering by local availability.

nodes.name: build_guidance_description
nodes.summary: Public entry that produces the cold-start guidance description for the configured experiment.

nodes.name: models_guidance_classified.json
nodes.summary: Cold-start knowledge base mapping task categories to recommended pretrained models with code templates and descriptions, used to seed initial solutions.

nodes.name: conditions.py
nodes.summary: Search-condition predicates determining when to trigger branch fusion and whether a branch or the whole search has become stagnant.

nodes.name: is_branch_stagnant
nodes.summary: Determines whether a given branch has stagnated based on recent improvement history and a threshold.

nodes.name: should_trigger_branch_fusion
nodes.summary: Decides whether conditions (time, successful nodes, branch count) warrant triggering a branch-fusion step.

nodes.name: is_globally_stagnant
nodes.summary: Determines whether the entire search has stagnated across all branches.

nodes.name: evaluation.py
nodes.summary: Node evaluation and reward propagation: computes node rewards, backpropagates values up the tree, and decides whether a child improved on its parent.

nodes.name: check_improvement
nodes.summary: Compares a node's metric against its parent to decide improvement, handling metric direction and validity, and updates node state accordingly.

nodes.name: backpropagate
nodes.summary: Propagates a reward value from a node up through its ancestors, updating visit counts and totals.

nodes.name: get_node_reward
nodes.summary: Computes the scalar reward for a node from its metric and validity state.

nodes.name: execution.py
nodes.summary: Thin validation layer over executed nodes: runs post-execution validation and logs the validation outcome.

nodes.name: validate_executed_node
nodes.summary: Validates a node after execution, marking buggy/valid state and logging the outcome.

nodes.name: executor.py
nodes.summary: Subprocess execution engine: the Interpreter runs candidate training scripts in isolated processes, integrates with the external GPU scheduler bridge, probes batch sizes, captures results and manages cleanup.
nodes.languageNotes: Large class managing subprocess lifecycle, scheduler IPC and resource isolation.

nodes.name: Interpreter
nodes.summary: Core executor that prepares and runs candidate scripts as subprocesses or scheduler jobs, attaches to the GPU scheduler service, probes/tunes resources, captures ExecutionResults and cleans up sessions.

nodes.name: ExecutionResult
nodes.summary: Dataclass holding the outcome of running a script: terminal output, execution time and exception type/info/stack.

nodes.name: _PreparedSchedulerJob
nodes.summary: Internal dataclass bundling a prepared scheduler job: node/process ids, run/result paths, runner kwargs and detected resource metadata (batch size, epochs, framework, GPU needs).

nodes.name: node_selection.py
nodes.summary: MCTS node-selection policy: UCT-based selection with time-decayed exploration constant, global top-k candidate gathering and a soft exploration/exploitation switch.

nodes.name: select_with_soft_switch
nodes.summary: Selects the next node using a time-dependent soft switch between exploration (UCT descent) and exploitation (weighted top-k).

nodes.name: get_top_k_nodes_global
nodes.summary: Gathers the global top-k candidate nodes across branches, capping per-branch contributions.

nodes.name: select
nodes.summary: Recursively descends the tree from a node using UCT to pick the most promising selectable child.

nodes.name: _compute_exploration_constant
nodes.summary: Computes the current UCT exploration constant via piecewise time decay.

nodes.name: get_exploration_weight
nodes.summary: Computes the exploration weight for the soft switch from elapsed/total time and switch bounds.

nodes.name: round_execution.py
nodes.summary: Dataclasses describing a parallel execution round: job specifications, job results and the hardware-tuning decision record passed between the search loop and executor.

nodes.name: RoundJobSpec
nodes.summary: Spec for a single round job: node id, code, task id, candidate metadata, hardware context and scheduler defaults.

nodes.name: RoundJobResult
nodes.summary: Result of a round job: node id, execution result, scheduler job id, profiling/probe/packing evidence and failure flag.

nodes.name: HardwareDecision
nodes.summary: Record of a hardware-tuning decision: stage, rationale, original vs chosen params, evidence refs, confidence and fallback reason, serializable to dict.

nodes.name: script_introspection.py
nodes.summary: Static introspection of candidate training scripts: regex/heuristic detectors for batch size, epochs, input resolution, folds, ensembles, TTA, model key, framework, AMP and GPU requirements feeding scheduler resource decisions.

nodes.name: introspect_training_script
nodes.summary: Runs the full suite of detectors over a training script and returns a consolidated signature of its resource characteristics.

nodes.name: detect_model_key
nodes.summary: Heuristically detects the pretrained model key/name referenced in a training script.

nodes.name: detect_framework
nodes.summary: Detects the deep-learning framework (e.g., torch/transformers) used by a training script.

nodes.name: infer_model_family
nodes.summary: Infers the model family from the detected model key and script contents.

nodes.name: search_node.py
nodes.summary: Core search-tree data structures: SearchNode (a solution candidate with code/metric/UCT/memory/hardware fields and tree operations) and Journal (the node container), plus path-filtering helpers.
nodes.languageNotes: Dataclass-based tree node with custom __eq__/__hash__/__getstate__ for pickling across processes.

nodes.name: SearchNode
nodes.summary: Represents one solution candidate in the search tree: holds code/plan/metric/execution results, UCT statistics, memory references and hardware-decision fields, and implements tree/UCT/beta-update operations and trajectory generation.

nodes.name: Journal
nodes.summary: Container of SearchNodes with helpers to append nodes and query draft/good/best nodes.

nodes.name: get_path_to_node
nodes.summary: Returns the node id path from the root to a given node within a journal.

nodes.name: filter_journal
nodes.summary: Filters a journal down to the relevant nodes (e.g., best/longest path) for downstream use.

nodes.name: solution_manager.py
nodes.summary: Persists search results: writes per-node metric files, copies the best solution and submission to disk, and maintains a per-branch top-K candidate ranking for the MLEvolve search engine.

nodes.name: write_metric_file
nodes.summary: Writes a human-readable metric report for a node (validation/test metric, stage, buggy flag) to a text file, respecting the metric-maximize direction.

nodes.name: save_best_solution
nodes.summary: Copies the winning node's code and submission into the best-solution directory and writes its plan and metric file.

nodes.name: update_top_candidates
nodes.summary: Adds a new node to the candidate pool, regroups candidates by branch, and recomputes the global top-K ranking sorted by reward.

nodes.name: save_top_candidates
nodes.summary: Serializes each ranked top candidate into its own rank directory, copying code, plan, metric file, and submission artifacts.

nodes.name: get_branch_top_nodes
nodes.summary: Returns the top-K nodes for a given branch sorted by reward, with diagnostic logging of the selection.

nodes.name: update_best_solution
nodes.summary: Compares a node against the current best solution, updates top candidates, and persists the new best when it improves the tracked metric.

nodes.name: _log_best_update
nodes.summary: Emits a pipeline event and records the best-solution update action for downstream monitoring.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package barrel re-exporting the validation submodule's public API: format-server client calls and submission quality-check helpers.

nodes.name: format_client.py
nodes.summary: HTTP client for the submission-format validation server: resolves server URLs, performs health checks with retry, and posts submission files for validation.

nodes.name: is_server_online
nodes.summary: Probes the validation server health endpoint with bounded retries, returning the live base URL or None when unreachable.

nodes.name: call_validate
nodes.summary: Uploads a submission file to the validation server's validate endpoint and returns the parsed JSON result, retrying on failure.

nodes.name: format_server.py
nodes.summary: Flask microservice exposing /validate and /health endpoints that run MLE-bench-style submission validation against a competition registry.
nodes.languageNotes: Flask route handlers using tempfile-backed upload handling and a competition registry lookup.

nodes.name: validate
nodes.summary: Flask handler that saves an uploaded submission to a temp file and validates it against the competition resolved from request headers.

nodes.name: quality_check.py
nodes.summary: Submission content-quality validation and LLM-assisted format repair: detects constant/empty/placeholder predictions and rewrites column headers to match the sample submission.

nodes.name: submission_format_fix_prompt
nodes.summary: Builds an LLM prompt comparing the submission's columns against the sample submission to request a corrected column mapping.

nodes.name: validate_submission_content_quality
nodes.summary: Inspects submission columns for degenerate predictions (single constant value, empties, placeholder patterns) and flags low-quality outputs.

nodes.name: _validate_submission_with_retry
nodes.summary: Calls the format-validation server and, on format failure, attempts an LLM-driven format fix before retrying up to a max attempt count.

nodes.name: try_fix_submission_format
nodes.summary: Uses an LLM column suggestion to rename submission columns and rewrite the CSV so it matches the expected sample format.

nodes.name: _extract_json_array
nodes.summary: Robustly extracts a JSON string array from LLM output, handling fenced code blocks, bare lines, and embedded arrays.

nodes.name: llm_suggest_submission_columns
nodes.summary: Generates a corrected column list for a submission by prompting the LLM and parsing its JSON array response.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: LLM facade that routes query/generate calls to the OpenAI-compatible or Gemini backend based on provider config and model name.

nodes.name: query
nodes.summary: Dispatches a system/user prompt with optional function spec to the selected backend (OpenAI or Gemini), logging prompt sizes and routing decisions.

nodes.name: generate
nodes.summary: Routes a free-form text generation request with optional JSON schema and retry policy to the selected backend.

nodes.name: gemini.py
nodes.summary: Google Gemini backend providing query (tool/function-calling) and generate (streaming) entry points, plus the shared FunctionSpec and prompt-to-markdown compiler.

nodes.name: backoff_create
nodes.summary: Generic exponential-backoff wrapper that retries a creation call on the given exception types.

nodes.name: compile_prompt_to_md
nodes.summary: Recursively flattens a string/list/dict prompt structure into a single Markdown document with nested headers.

nodes.name: FunctionSpec
nodes.summary: Dataclass describing an LLM tool/function (name, JSON schema, description) with helpers to emit OpenAI tool dicts and Gemini tool declarations.

nodes.name: query
nodes.summary: Calls the Gemini generate_content API with optional function calling and thinking config, parsing and validating the structured tool response.

nodes.name: generate
nodes.summary: Streams a text generation from Gemini with retry/backoff, optional thinking budget and JSON schema, returning the assembled output.

nodes.name: model_profiles.py
nodes.summary: Capability registry for LLM models: declares thinking/JSON-schema support and resolves per-model parameter profiles by name prefix.

nodes.name: get_profile
nodes.summary: Resolves the parameter profile for a model by matching the longest known name prefix and optionally merging thinking-mode settings.

nodes.name: openai.py
nodes.summary: OpenAI-compatible backend (Qwen / OpenRouter / any compatible endpoint) implementing query with function calling and generate with streaming, plus robust JSON-argument parsing.

nodes.name: _parse_json_args
nodes.summary: Parses tool-call argument JSON robustly, stripping markdown fences and normalizing malformed multi-line string values before loading.

nodes.name: _extract_json_object
nodes.summary: Extracts the first balanced JSON object from free-form text, handling fenced code blocks and brace matching.

nodes.name: query
nodes.summary: Issues a chat completion with optional tool forcing, applies per-model profile parameters, and parses the tool-call or content JSON response.

nodes.name: _prompt_to_messages
nodes.summary: Converts a structured prompt (string or dict with system/user roles) into an OpenAI chat-messages list, accounting for models without system-role support.

nodes.name: generate
nodes.summary: Streams a chat-completion text generation with retry/backoff, optional JSON schema and thinking-mode handling per model profile.

nodes.name: run.py
nodes.summary: Main entry point orchestrating an MLEvolve search run: bootstraps config/logging/hardware monitor, builds the agent and interpreter, attaches the scheduler, and drives coldstart drafts and the concurrent search loop.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses ThreadPoolExecutor windows, signal handling for graceful SIGTERM shutdown, and atexit cleanup; ends with os._exit to force termination.

nodes.name: _scheduler_settings_from_cfg
nodes.summary: Builds SchedulerSettings from the run config, preferring inline settings then a settings file, with fallback to defaults.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: End-to-end run driver: sets up the workspace, agent, interpreter and scheduler, executes coldstart draft nodes and the main concurrent search window, then persists results and tears down resources.

nodes.name: SignalShutdown
nodes.summary: Exception subclass raised on SIGTERM to trigger graceful shutdown of the search loop.

layers.name: 入口与编排层
layers.description: 顶层入口，run.py 编排整轮搜索流程，__init__.py 暴露 Experiment 类初始化配置、Journal、agent 与解释器并驱动 SearchNode 树搜索循环。

layers.name: 搜索引擎层
layers.description: MLEvolve 的核心树搜索循环：agent_search 主循环、search_node 节点模型、node_selection 选择策略、executor/execution/round_execution 代码执行、evaluation 指标评估、coldstart 冷启动知识与 validation 校验子系统。

layers.name: 代码生成 Agent 层
layers.description: draft/improve/debug/fusion/evolution/aggregation/code_review 等候选代码生成 Agent，及其支撑的 coder（含 diff_coder 补丁应用）、planner 规划、prompts 提示模板、memory 记忆检索与 hardware_context 硬件上下文模块。

layers.name: LLM 集成层
layers.description: 统一的 LLM provider 外观接口与 Gemini、OpenAI 适配器及 model_profiles 模型档案，被搜索引擎依赖以驱动各生成 Agent 的模型调用。

layers.name: 配置层
layers.description: 基于 Hydra 的配置加载模块（config/__init__.py）与示例运行配置 config.example.yaml，定义实验运行的可调参数。

layers.name: 基础设施与工具层
layers.description: 运行与部署支撑：bootstrap/start/install/nautilus 等 shell 脚本、docker-compose.local.yml 与 .devcontainer 容器定义、.github 的 dependabot CI 配置及 .understandignore 工具配置。

layers.name: 文档层
layers.description: 项目说明与开发约定文档：README、CLAUDE、TODO 指南与 requirements_base.txt 依赖清单。

tour.title: 项目概览
tour.description: 从 README 开始了解 MLEvolve 是什么：一个基于蒙特卡洛图搜索（Monte Carlo Graph Search）的智能体式机器学习工程系统，能在 MLE-bench 任务上自动演化出 ML 解决方案，并集成了硬件感知（hardware-aware）能力。README 还给出了 MLE-bench 成绩、环境搭建与快速启动步骤，是理解后续所有代码的总览地图。

tour.title: 实验编排入口
tour.description: run.py 是整个系统的命令行入口，负责加载配置、构造并驱动一整轮搜索流程；顶层包的 __init__.py 则定义了 Experiment 编排器与 Solution 数据类，它初始化配置、Journal、各 Agent 与代码解释器，运行若干步搜索循环后抽取最优节点作为最终解。这两个文件让你对系统运行时结构有鸟瞰式认识。
tour.languageLesson: __init__.py 中使用了 ModuleNotFoundError 的惰性回退导入模式（lazy fallback import），让可选依赖在缺失时不会直接导致包导入失败，这是 Python 处理可选依赖的常见做法。

tour.title: 搜索引擎主循环
tour.description: engine/agent_search.py 是搜索引擎的核心，也是依赖关系上扇出（fan-out）最高的枢纽模块——它把节点选择、评估、执行与停止条件等子系统串联成一轮树搜索循环。承接第 2 步 Experiment 的驱动，本步揭示「每一步搜索究竟做了什么」：选择一个节点、调用 Agent 生成候选、执行并评估、再回写树。

tour.title: 搜索树：Journal 与 SearchNode
tour.description: engine/search_node.py 定义了 Journal/SearchNode 树结构，是全项目被依赖最多（fan-in 最高）的模块——node_selection、evaluation、execution 都建立在它之上。每个 SearchNode 承载一份候选代码、执行结果与评分，Journal 则组织这些节点构成可搜索的图。理解了这棵树，才能理解后续所有 Agent 是如何在树上「长出」新解的。

tour.title: 代码执行与评估
tour.description: 搜索树上的每个候选都需要真正跑起来才能打分。executor.py 负责调度并行执行轮次（配合 round_execution.py 描述的作业规格与硬件调优记录），script_introspection 与 execution.py 做执行后校验，evaluation.py 则计算节点奖励、沿树反向传播价值并判断子节点是否优于父节点。这构成了搜索的「评估—反馈」闭环。

tour.title: 冷启动知识与候选验证
tour.description: 在第一份草稿生成之前，engine/coldstart/knowledge.py 会按竞赛标签从分类知识库注入领域先验（competition_tag / models_guidance 两份 JSON 即知识来源）。validation 子系统则在执行前后把关：format_client/format_server 校验提交格式，quality_check 借助 LLM 做质量检查。二者一头一尾地保障了搜索的有效性。

tour.title: 演化 Agent：草稿、改进、调试
tour.description: 这里是 MLEvolve 名字的来源。draft_agent 从零（或种子文件）产出初始解作为搜索树的根；improve_agent 在已有父解上做增量精炼；debug_agent 则诊断执行失败的节点、检索过往相似修复并生成修正版本。三者都通过第 4 步的接口把新解注册为搜索树上的新节点，是树「生长」的主要动力。

tour.title: 演化 Agent：融合、跃迁、聚合
tour.description: 当单分支增量改进陷入停滞时，更大尺度的演化算子登场：evolution_agent 在分支停滞时触发架构级改动，fusion_agent 将两个或多个高分分支解合并为一个新节点，aggregation_agent 则收集各分支代表节点综合出统一解。conditions.py（第 3 步）提供的停滞/融合判定正是触发它们的开关。

tour.title: Coder 与 Diff 补丁子系统
tour.description: 所有 Agent 最终都委托 coder 把「计划」变成「代码」：base_coder 做单次 plan-and-code 查询，stepwise_coder 把生成拆成数据/模型/训练等步骤再由元代理合并。改进与演化类 Agent 偏好 diff_coder——以 SEARCH/REPLACE 补丁块对父代码做最小改动，由 patcher.py 做缩进感知的模糊匹配应用、apply.py 在失败时重试再生。这解释了树上节点为何能「继承并微调」父代码。
tour.languageLesson: diff_coder 采用 SEARCH/REPLACE 补丁格式而非整段重写：让 LLM 只输出需要变更的代码片段，既节省 token 又降低破坏既有正确代码的风险；patcher 通过空白归一化与缩进迁移做容错匹配，是 LLM 代码编辑工程中的常见健壮性手段。

tour.title: Planner、Prompts 与 Memory
tour.description: 支撑 Agent 决策的三个横向模块：planner 先规划再编码，planner_with_memory 借助检索到的过往成功/失败记录精炼出校验过的 JSON 计划；prompts 包统一管理防泄漏、响应格式、环境与实现指南等提示模板；memory 层（global_memory + 混合检索 retriever + 嵌入模型）持久化成功与缺陷节点记录，并在调试和改进时召回相似经验作为指引。

tour.title: 硬件感知与 LLM 集成层
tour.description: hardware_context.py 是项目的差异化亮点：它向调度器查询 VRAM/运行时画像，压缩证据后生成「硬件设计简报」与提示片段，注入到每个 Agent 阶段与搜索节点，并据此重写 batch size、epochs 等训练参数以贴合可用显存。所有 LLM 调用则统一经由 llm/__init__.py 外观接口分发到 gemini/openai 适配器，model_profiles 维护各模型档案。

tour.title: 配置、部署与运行环境
tour.description: 收尾理解系统如何被配置与跑起来。config/__init__.py 用 dataclass 层级定义全部可调参数，config.example.yaml 是其镜像模板。docker-compose.local.yml 拉起 memory 与调度上下文依赖的 Qdrant 向量库与 Neo4j 图库；bootstrap.sh 在启动时拉起数据库、等待就绪并注入 schema 知识；nautilus.sh 则把同一套栈部署到 Kubernetes 集群。至此你已掌握 MLEvolve 从配置、依赖、搜索到部署的完整链路。
tour.languageLesson: config.example.yaml 是一个深度嵌套的 YAML 映射，逐字镜像 config/__init__.py 中的 dataclass 层级（Config → ExperimentConfig / AgentConfig / ExecConfig / SchedulerBridgeConfig…），这是 OmegaConf/Hydra 风格配置的典型做法：用代码里的 dataclass 做类型契约，用 YAML 做可覆盖的实例值。
